zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Haydock於1397年出生在英國藍卡爾艾克斯頓時，她的父親Gilbert Haydock24歲，母親Isabel de Houghton12歲。她於1414年與Sir Piers Legh II, of Lyme Hall在英國英格蘭藍卡爾布拉德利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1440年1月17日在英國藍卡爾可朗特享年43歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾澤福頓。

nameMeanings.name: Haydock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Czech or Polish Hejduk , Polish, Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia and Poland), or Czech Hajduk , and of their Hungarian cognate Hajdók. Compare Hydock .

variants.name: Haddock
meanings: variant of the Lancashire surname Haydock , a habitational name from a place so called near Liverpool, which is probably named from Welsh heiddog ‘characterized by barley’.
from the Middle English personal name Addoc, a pet form of Old English Æddi (itself perhaps derived from one of the names in ēad ‘wealth, prosperity’), with prosthetic H-.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Haydock was born in 1397, in Eccleston, Lancashire, England, her father, Gilbert Haydock, was 24 and her mother, Isabel de Houghton, was 12. She married Sir Piers Legh II, of Lyme Hall in 1414, in Bradley, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 17 January 1440, in Croxteth, Lancashire, England, at the age of 43, and was buried in Sefton, Lancashire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Haydock(이)가 1397년 영국 랭카실르 치레스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert Haydock(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel de Houghton(은)는 12세였다. 그녀는 1414년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 브래들리에서 Sir Piers Legh II, of Lyme Hall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1440년 1월 17일 영국 랭카실르 크록스테스에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 세프턴에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Piers Legh II, of Lyme Hall em 1414, em Bradley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. ru.Macclesfield St Michael, Cheshire, Engla: макклесфиелд ст мачаел, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Haydock родилась в 1397 г. в местности экклестон, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Gilbert Haydock было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Houghton, 12. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Piers Legh II, of Lyme Hall в 1414 г. в местности брадли, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 17 января 1440 г. в местности крокстет, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности сефтон, ланкашир, Англия.
